what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry for the application's surface layer. It records the designed intent, build status, and compositional relationships of per-surface skeletons, with a depth-first treatment of the Interactive Addressed Surface criteria, a universal Sequences primitive, and the Information Architecture as the parent container that places other surfaces.
observations: Uses an explicit honesty contract with four tags: DESIGNED, BUILT, DESIGNED-☐, and INFERRED.
Part 1 is a depth-first pass over the Interactive Addressed Surface, organized into criterion groups: Prerequisite, Spine, Form, Interaction, Resolution, Blueprint, and Loops Closed.
Each criterion is assigned a status relative to code-truth on main, not relative to the Criteria's own ☐ markers.
The document explicitly resolves a source conflict about S3 (resolve_scope) by trusting a newer code-truth read over an older research synthesis.
Part 2 enumerates roughly 17 surfaces, each with intent, built reality, and composes notes.
Part 3 frames the Sequences primitive as a single loop — resolve → present/work → persist → next/trigger — recurring across engine, walkthrough, and cognition.
Part 4 presents the IA as a parent container with a CSS-grid layout and a relationship graph showing which surfaces are placed inside which zones.
Includes a naming note distinguishing Collective Cognition from Concurrent Cognition and flags unverified items for a next wave.
Contains no code symbols; the symbols list is explicitly empty.
standouts: The click-indicates+consents model: a click is attention and consent to consider, not execution; the RHM proposes and governance/see-and-approve decides.
The IA-as-parent / simultaneity principle: the same ui:// address is meant to be visible simultaneously on the board, inspector, and RHM pane.
The Sequences primitive is treated as the same loop operating at engine, walkthrough/review, and collective-cognition scales.
The resolved S3 source conflict and the insistence on deriving BUILT/DESIGNED-☐ from live function bodies rather than from stale checkboxes.
The studio surface is described as superseded by a rebuild as an in-app page reusing the real RHM organ.
conventions: Every claim is tagged with one of DESIGNED, BUILT, DESIGNED-☐, or INFERRED.
Sources are named per section and often per row.
Criteria are identified by short IDs (S0, F1, I3, L2, etc.) and paired with test names.
Tables organize status, intent, and verification.
ASCII diagrams illustrate layout and surface relationships.
The document is explicit about staleness, verification limits, and what was not read.
concerns: The ledger mixes design specification, build tracker, and architecture map in one long document, which may be hard to keep current.
Many claims rely on inferred synthesis or on sources that the file says were not fully read.
Status labels are time-sensitive; the file itself notes that a synthesis can become stale within days.
Some criteria statuses depend on manual resolution of conflicting sources.
notes: A design-intent ledger for the addressed UI surface, mapping which interaction primitives are already built on main versus still designed, and situating them inside the IA-as-parent architecture.
questions: Which exact commit or branch is considered 'main' for the BUILT claims?
What is the precise schema of the canonical ui:// address grammar intended by S0?
How is the DESIGNED-☐ status verified independently of the Completion Criteria's own ☐ markers?
What happens when an address has conflicting governance tiers across different registries?
How does the mobile IA preserve co-resolution while foregrounding only one zone at a time?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file states it is adding a neutral, file-local, non-ranking interpretive layer for a ledger of code/design substrates, and it does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Simultaneity and co-resolution of the same address
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a trustworthy, verifiable map between design intent and implemented code for the surface layer, so that future build decisions can be grounded in what is actually built and what remains designed-only, while unifying all surfaces under an addressed, governed interaction model.
novelty: The explicit four-tag honesty contract applied as a ledger methodology, the treatment of the IA as a 'parent surface' whose value is simultaneity of the same address across multiple zones, and the universal Sequences primitive tying engine, walkthrough, and cognition together.
contribution: A structured, tagged inventory of surface design intent versus build status; the addressed-interaction governance model; the IA-as-parent simultaneity principle; the Sequences-as-universal-loop framing; and a methodology for distinguishing designed, built, and inferred claims.
summary_for_embedding: A design-intent ledger mapping the addressed UI surface criteria, per-surface skeletons, the sequences primitive, and the IA-as-parent layout, tagged by built/designed/inferred status.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a neutral, verifiable interpretive layer that records which surface interaction primitives are designed versus built and how they compose under the addressed-surface architecture.
